verbGlossary.CONTROLS: {source} governs access, activity sequencing, and safety compliance across the entire project site on a daily basis.
verbGlossary.SECURES: {source} establishes and enforces site-wide safety perimeters, access controls, and hazard mitigation measures.
verbGlossary.OVERSEES: {target} maintains high-level accountability for all activity at {source}, reviewing progress and resolving escalations.
verbGlossary.PATROLS: {source} walks a building or zone on a recurring basis to observe work quality, crew activity, and safety conditions.
verbGlossary.INSPECTS: {source} physically examines {target} against quality acceptance criteria, punch list requirements, or code compliance standards.
verbGlossary.BARRICADES: {source} installs or directs the placement of physical barriers, signage, and fall protection around exterior hazard zones.
verbGlossary.STAGES: {source} positions crews, materials, and equipment within {target} in preparation for the day's scheduled work.
verbGlossary.ALLOCATES: {source} designates laydown yard space and staging areas to specific trades, deliveries, or equipment based on the logistics plan.
verbGlossary.SURVEYS: {target} performs field measurements, grade checks, or layout verification at {source} using instruments or reference points.
verbGlossary.SEQUENCES: {source} determines the floor-by-floor or zone-by-zone order in which trades mobilize, install, and demobilize.
verbGlossary.MOBILIZES: {source} moves crew, tools, and materials to a specific floor or zone to begin a scheduled work activity.
verbGlossary.MONITORS: {target} maintains ongoing surveillance of {source} for hazardous conditions, restricted-space compliance, or environmental risks.
verbGlossary.BENCHMARKS: {source} establishes elevation control points and survey benchmarks at below-grade locations to guide excavation and foundation work.
verbGlossary.PERMITS: {source} issues or verifies hot work, confined space, or fall protection permits required before crews access a high-risk location.
verbGlossary.RIGS: {source} sets up hoisting, scaffolding, or fall arrest systems at roof level prior to crew access for roofing or mechanical work.
verbGlossary.COORDINATES: {source} sequences multiple trades working in the same zone or wing to prevent stacking, conflicts, and rework.
verbGlossary.MODELS: {source} creates or updates the BIM/VDC model for a specific location to detect clashes before installation begins.
verbGlossary.DETECTS: {source} identifies above-ceiling coordination conflicts, routing clashes, or clearance violations during a plenum walkthrough or model review.
verbGlossary.PUNCHLISTS: {source} generates and tracks the room-by-room punch list of deficiencies that must be corrected before substantial completion.
verbGlossary.WATERPROOFS: {source} inspects wet-area installations (membranes, drain slopes, tile adhesion) against waterproofing specifications before concealment.
verbGlossary.FINISHES: {source} installs or touches up wall surfaces, paint, trim, and final finishes to meet closeout quality standards.
verbGlossary.FIRESTOPS: {source} inspects penetrations and openings for compliant firestopping sealant, wrap, or pillow installation per the fire-rated assembly.
verbGlossary.VERIFIES: {source} confirms that a fire or smoke compartment meets code-required ratings through visual inspection and documentation review.
verbGlossary.DEMARCATES: {source} defines the physical boundaries and access rules for each work zone or phase area on the project schedule.
verbGlossary.COMMISSIONS: {source} performs functional performance testing of building systems within a designated commissioning zone.
verbGlossary.BALANCES: {source} adjusts airflow, water flow, or electrical loads within a commissioning zone to meet design specifications.
verbGlossary.TRACKS: {target} monitors {source}-based quality or safety metric over time and flags deviations from acceptable thresholds.
verbGlossary.CORRELATES WITH: {target}measure has a statistical relationship with {source} staffing metric across the portfolio.
